As well, let's talk a little bit about that. And by the way, in this, we have an actual script that I believe you wrote for what the staff should say to generate referrals for you. Yeah, it's a direct ask. It's a direct ask. Right. Yeah, it's a direct ask. So let's talk about training. Because like we talked about it earlier, I think in segment one. Look, if they are between you and your customer and the referrals that come from your customer, then they've got to be in the game. And in many businesses, they have more opportunity to influence this than the owner or the practitioner anyway. In a dental office, the front desk staff, the hygienists actually have more opportunity to influence this than the doctor does. So as with all sales management. Which is what it is. You've got to have a process. It's got to be taught, coached, monitored, enforced, and rewarded or punished based on compliance. Yeah, so again. And so you can't let this be random and casual and expect anything but random and casual results. Now you can force these conversations. The button. Ask me. Ask me about headache cures. I mean, you can force it. Signage. You can do a lot of things in a physical environment. But still, that person has to, hey, look, did you get this month's referral cards for the report? Here, let me give you six of them. I mean, it has to happen. And it just falls in with anything else. If you have an upsell script, if you have whatever they're supposed to be doing, if you're not teaching, coaching, monitoring, and enforcing, you're not going to get it. Right, exactly. So again, I just want to show everybody. So this is in your manual. It's called the direct ask. Direct ask. And there's an actual script. Say that 10 times fast. Yeah, right. Maybe earlier. I mean, it needs some more sugar to do that. I'm going to need some more sugar. But the direct ask script, again, that's right in your manual. So you want to make sure that you check that out. And again, you've got to train the staff on how to use it. You've got to practice. You've got to drill. You've got to rehearse with them and make sure that they get it done. All right, so let's move on to something really cool, which I would make a bet that most people aren't doing. But if you do it, it's very powerful, which are client events, client referral events. Talk a little bit about those, because boy. Well, so everybody's seen some version of them, usually not done well. Fashion retailers or jewelry retailers will occasionally have the trunk sale day. The department stores do. And the factory rep is there. RV dealers will advertise. They'll have their own little two-day show. And the factory reps from these five different brands of RVs are going to be here. So they will publicly advertise these things. And they will advertise them to their customers. What they don't do is really make them about, here's a thing to bring other people to. And they also aren't very good about doing the non-sales activity event for people to bring other people to. So we have a member, for example, who's in a dairy business, Alan Reed. And so Alan has a dairy. He's got cows and milk and machines and stuff. And they have home delivery. So I think he's got 3,000, 3,200, some good-sized number of customers who their trucks deliver milk and ice cream stuff to. And they have a store, a retail store. And so once a year, they have farm days at the farm. And all the customers are encouraged, bring family, bring friends, invite people from out of, whatever you want to do. Our farm is your farm. And for these two days, there's picnic area.
